title: Pepinia alborubra
intro: Pepinia alborubra, also known as the white-stemmed pepinia, is a species of flowering plant in the asparagus family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pepinia alborra include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Pepinia alborubra is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used as a treatment for fever and respiratory infections, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Pepinia alborubra is classified within the family Annonaceae, which includes various species of flowering plants commonly known as custard apples or sugar apples. This classification places Pepinia alborubra among the over 200 genera within the family Annonaceae.
